author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, John. xxi. 16, ‘Simon, son of John, lovest thou me?’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, John. xxi. 16, ‘Simon, son of John, lovest thou me?’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have observed, beloved, that in to-day’s lesson it was said by the Lord to Peter in a question, “Lovest thou Me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To whom he answered, “Thou knowest, Lord, that I love thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This was done a second, and a third time; and at each several reply, the Lord said, “Feed My lambs.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To Peter did Christ commend His lambs to be fed, who fed even Peter himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what could Peter do for the Lord, especially now that He had an Immortal Body, and was about to ascend into heaven?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As though He had said to him, “‘Lovest thou Me?’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Herein show that thou lovest Me, ‘Feed my sheep.’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then, Brethren, do ye with obedience hear that ye are Christ’s sheep; seeing that we on our part with fear hear, Feed My sheep”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If we feed with fear, and fear for the sheep; these sheep how ought they to fear for themselves?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let then carefulness be our portion, obedience yours; pastoral watchfulness our portion, the humility of the flock yours.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Although we too who seem to speak to you from a higher place, are with fear beneath your feet; forasmuch as we know how perilous an account must be rendered of this as it were exalted seat.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore, dearly beloved, Catholic plants, Members of Christ, think What a Head ye have!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Children of God, think What a Father ye have found.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christians, think What an Inheritance is promised you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not such as on earth cannot be possessed by children, save when their parents are dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For no one on earth possesses a father’s inheritance, save when he is dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But we whilst our Father liveth shall possess what He shall give; for that our Father cannot die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I add more, I say more, and say the truth; our Father will Himself be our Inheritance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Live consistently, especially ye candidates of Christ, recently baptized, just regenerated, as I have admonished you before, so say I now, and give expression to my solicitude; for the present lesson of the Gospel hath forced upon me a greater fear: take heed to yourselves, do not imitate evil Christians.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Say not I will do this, for many of the faithful do it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is not to procure a defence for the soul; but to look out for companions unto hell.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Grow ye in this floor of the Lord; herein ye will find good men to please you, if ye yourselves are good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For are ye our private property?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Heretics and schismatics have made their own private property out of what they have stolen from the Lord, and would feed, not Christ’s flocks, but their own against Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is true indeed, they place His title on these their spoils, that their robberies may be as it were maintained by the title of His Power.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What doeth Christ when such as these are converted, who have received the title of His Baptism out of the Church?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He casteth out the spoiler, He doth not efface the title, and taketh possession of the house; because He hath found His title there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What need is there that He should change His Own Name?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do they take heed to what the Lord said to Peter, “Feed My lambs, feed My sheep”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Did He say to him, “Feed thy lambs;” or, “Feed thy sheep”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But for them who are shut out, what said He in the Song of Songs, unto the Church?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Spouse speaking to the Bride, saith, “If thou know not thyself, O thou fair one among women, go forth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As though He said, “I do not cast thee out, ‘go forth, if thou know not thyself, O thou fair one among women,’ if thou know not thyself in the mirror of divine Scripture, if thou give not heed, O thou fair woman, to the mirror which with no false lustre deceiveth thee; if thou know not that of thee it is said, ‘Thy glory shall be above all earth;’ that of thee it is said, ‘I will give thee nations for thine inheritance, and the limits of the earth for thy possession;’ and other innumerable testimonies which set forth the Catholic Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then thou know not these, thou hast no part in Me, thou canst not make thyself My heir.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘Go forth then in the footsteps of the flocks’ not in the fellowship of the flock; and feed thy goats, not as it was said to Peter, ‘My sheep.’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To Peter it was said, “My sheep;” to schismatics it is said,” thy goats.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the one place “sheep,” in the other “goats;” in the one place “Mine,” in the other “thine.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Recollect the right Hand and the left of our Judge; recollect where the goats shall stand, and where the sheep; and it will be plain to you where is the right hand, where the left, the white and the black, the lightsome, and the darksome, the fair and the deformed, that which is about to receive the kingdom, and that which is to find everlasting punishment.
